title: Seer
text: Home * Engines * Seer Greek Seer [1] Seer , an UCI compliant open source chess engine by Connor McMonigle , written in C++ , licensed under the GNU GPL and first released in October 2020. Seer uses a custom NNUE implementation based on 32-bit float weights with training code written in PyTorch and inference code relying on OpenMP SIMD for auto vectorization [2] . Due to PEXT Bitboards , Seer requires BMI2 for a reasonable performance, as well as either SSE , AVX , AVX2 or AVX-512 to calculate the NNUE. More recently, Seer supports ARM NEON via sse2neon [3] to run on many Android devices [4] .
